authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service (Humboldt-Toiyabe National Forest, Carson-Iceberg Wilderness)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(137) — Silver King Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.)
sources.quotedText: (137) Silver King Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.) upstream of the confluence with Snodgrass Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Yubanet — Paiute Cutthroat Trout Return to Silver King Creek for First Time in 71 Years
sources.quotedText: On Aug. 24, 2017, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service biologists, in conjunction with members of the U.S. Forest Service (USFS) and the California Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW), released 86 Paiute cutthroat trout in Silver King Creek above Llewellyn Falls

sources.title: Carson Now — Paiute Cutthroat Trout Reintroduced to High Sierra Wilderness Native Habitat
sources.quotedText: 30 Paiute cutthroat trout of varying sizes were released into Silver King Creek in Alpine County, Calif., Sept. 18, 2019.

sources.title: Paiute cutthroat trout recovery effort continues | US Forest Service
sources.quotedText: translocation efforts into the mainstem of Silver King Creek will need to continue yearly until a self-sustaining population has been established.

parameters.note: Closure protects the native Paiute cutthroat trout, whose entire historical range was Silver King Creek above a barrier falls upstream of the confluence with Snodgrass Creek; CDFW has been actively restoring the species to this reach.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year on Silver King Creek and tributaries upstream of the confluence with Snodgrass Creek
regulations.verbatimText: Silver King Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.) upstream of the confluence with Snodgrass Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: USFS/CDFW/USFWS translocate Paiute cutthroat trout from donor populations (e.g. North Fork Cottonwood Creek, Coyote Valley Creek, Corral Valley Creek) into Silver King Creek as an ongoing native-species restoration effort; the forest's fisheries biologist states translocations 'will need to continue yearly until a self-sustaining population has been established.'
